title: thevetia neriifolia
intro: Thevetia neriifolia, also known as the yellow oleander, is a poisonous plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's toxic compound, thevetin, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of thevetia neriifolia include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural insecticide and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of thevetia neriifolia can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, but it's essential to consult with a medical professional before using any part of the plant due to its toxicity.
